title: stillingia linearifolia var. paucidentata
intro: Stillingia linearifolia var. paucidentata, also known as a variety of the Queen's Root plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Stillingia linearifolia var. paucidentata include reducing swelling and pain, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
